subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2026, EC, OECD, Eurostat, WJP, RSF, TI, DBRS) + Luxembourg official (STATEC) + press.
A tiny, ultra-wealthy, AAA-rated eurozone financial-hub state with strong institutions; a vast non-bank fund sector is the main systemic-exposure flag.

Standout features: AAA fiscal strength, top-tier institutions, and an open EU-core role, against finance-sector concentration, housing-cost strain, and a residual tax-secrecy reputation. Source-lean: LOW-MODERATE Anglophone.

economic.notes: AAA fiscal strength and a high-value-added economy, with finance-sector concentration the systemic risk.

fiscal_state.notes: AAA-rated with very low debt (~26% GDP), though a 2024 surplus flipped to a ~2% deficit and debt is not yet stabilising. (IMF Art IV 2026; DBRS)

monetary_independence.notes: Pooled at the ECB - credible but not sovereign. (ECB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: A euro participant in the world's second reserve currency. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: Tracking the eurozone (~2.2% easing toward ~1.5%). (STATEC; EC)

banking_stability.notes: Sound and well-capitalised, but with a vast non-bank/investment-fund sector posing liquidity-mismatch/leverage interlinkages. (IMF FSAP 2024)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A world-leading fund domicile plus steel and space/satellite - high value-added but finance-concentrated. (OECD; IMF)

institutional.notes: Top-tier institutions, with lobbying/conflict-of-interest a TI-flagged blind spot.

rule_of_law.notes: Among the world's strongest (a WJP top-10 standing). (WJP 2025)

civil_service_capacity.notes: High administrative quality, an EU-institution host. (OECD; EC)

press_freedom.notes: Free (RSF ~9th), with small-market concentration and a TI-flagged lobbying blind spot. (RSF; TI 2025)

political_social.notes: A very stable democracy, very open to foreign nationals, with housing-cost strain the caveat.

political_stability.notes: A 'full democracy' with an orderly 2023 handover. (Economist; Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: ~47% of residents are foreign nationals - a very open EU labour hub, though housing costs push some out. (STATEC; OECD)

social_cohesion.notes: Cohesive at very high incomes, with acute housing-affordability strain. (STATEC)

demographics.notes: Immigration-fuelled growth (slowing), with low native fertility. (STATEC; Eurostat)

geopolitical.notes: A reliable NATO/EU founder and institution host with an open account.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO and EU founding member and EU-institution host, with defence rising fast. (NATO; STATEC)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An EU regime with an open capital account; a mild residual tax-secrecy reputational legacy. (EC; TI)

physical_practical.notes: Excellent healthcare, infrastructure, and safety, with low catastrophe exposure.

climate.notes: Wealthy, top-readiness temperate, with modest heat and Moselle/river-flood exposure; an AMOC tail caveat. (ND-GAIN; EEA)

disaster_insurance.notes: A developed market with low catastrophe exposure. (Munich Re)

summary: A tiny, ultra-wealthy AAA-rated eurozone state with among the world's strongest institutions, excellent healthcare and infrastructure, low crime, very low public debt, and an exceptionally open, high-income, foreign-majority labour market. Its economy is a high-value-added fund-management and financial-services hub, which is also its main vulnerability: a vast non-bank fund sector poses liquidity-mismatch and contagion risk, and a residual tax-secrecy reputation lingers. Housing-cost strain and a 2025 fiscal slip are the domestic watch-items. A reliable NATO/EU founder and institution host.
